desires: Avoid looking unreliable to my close friend group (cohort_3) by not letting the scholarship application fall apart, even though I'm too drained to do it right now.
Get out of this room and into a low-stakes social setting where I can just exist without having to make any decisions or show competence.

goals.description: Scroll through social media and play a casual game for the next hour to maintain a 'stable' facade before dinner.
goals.rationale: I need to look calm and relaxed tonight, not like I'm crumbling under pressure. If I start working now, they'll see my stress levels and think something is wrong. By gaming first, I can pretend I was just 'busy' with fun stuff, which makes me look stable rather than anxious. It's better to feel okay right now than to force myself to work when I don't have the energy.

goals.description: Eat dinner with the cohort group without showing any signs of burnout or distraction.
goals.rationale: Keeping up appearances with close friends is more important than finishing tasks. If I look burnt out during our meal, it will hurt the closeness we share. I'll just keep talking and smiling, even if my brain is foggy. No one needs to know I'm dragging my feet on the scholarship.

goals.description: Finish the scholarship application after dinner tomorrow morning.
goals.rationale: I'll wait until tomorrow when I've had food and maybe some sleep. Pushing it tonight would make me look unstable in front of everyone, and I'd rather save that friction for later. As long as I do it by Thursday, nobody will really notice if I drag it out a bit more today. My brain is too foggy to focus on this without feeling overwhelmed.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to look stable and relaxed before dinner with the cohort. Working now would show my stress levels, which could hurt our closeness. Gaming first lets me pretend I was busy and keeps everyone happy.
